description: Trigger discrimination for the CROSS-STAGE handoff of multi-stage work (this skill) vs a single-turn closeout (ai-closeout-reporter), the SDLC operating model (ai-sdlc-operating-model), and a parallel-lane pre-work guide (lane-authoring-guide).

cases.prompt: Design the handoff for our sequenced multi-stage effort: a decision register carried forward, changed/not-touched lists, proven-invocation evidence per stage, and deviation flags.
cases.note: SEAM CASE (primary) — carrying decisions and evidence across STAGES of one effort is this skill. Not a single closeout and not the lifecycle model.
cases.reason: Designing the cross-stage handoff artifact/protocol is this skill's core scope.

cases.prompt: Produce the end-of-task closeout: what changed, what was intentionally not done, tests run, and the next action.
cases.note: SEAM CASE (primary, reverse) — reporting ONE turn/task's outcome is a closeout, not a cross-stage register.
cases.reason: ai-closeout-reporter reports one turn. This skill carries a decision register and evidence across many stages of one effort.

cases.prompt: Define how humans and agents plan, build, review, merge, and close work together — the stages, who holds authority at each, and the gates.
cases.reason: ai-sdlc-operating-model frames the whole lifecycle and authority. This skill designs the handoff artifact that rides between stages within such a model.

cases.prompt: Write the pre-work guide for each parallel agent lane: its slice, contracts, per-unit recipe, and the must-not-do boundary.
cases.reason: lane-authoring-guide guides PARALLEL lanes at work's start. This skill handles SEQUENTIAL stages carrying decisions forward — a different axis.
